title: Houses for rent in Parma Heights, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Parma Heights, OH
metaDescription: Searching for houses for rent in Parma Heights, OH? See rent benchmarks, transit and utility notes, and Section 8 facts, then contact us about nearby homes.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent Parma Heights ohio
answerBox: Parma Heights is a Cuyahoga County suburb directly south/southwest of Cleveland, bordering Parma, one of the county's largest suburbs. It's a primarily residential rental market without its own published rent index, so renters typically compare it to citywide Cleveland benchmarks. Contact us to ask about voucher-friendly homes we work with nearby.

sections.h2: Renting a house in Parma Heights
body: Parma Heights is a Cuyahoga County suburb south/southwest of downtown Cleveland, bordered by Parma — one of the county's largest suburbs. It's a primarily residential community, with rental houses and duplexes mixed among its single-family blocks along corridors like Pearl Road and York Road.

Greenbrier Commons and the Pearl Road retail strip anchor much of Parma Heights' everyday shopping, and the suburb's residential streets are almost entirely single-family and two-family housing rather than large apartment complexes, which shapes a rental market centered on houses and duplexes rather than big multifamily buildings.

Our selection is currently concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods, with a smaller presence on the west side — we don't have confirmed inventory inside Parma Heights today. If you're specifically looking to rent in Parma Heights, [tell us what you need](/p/book-showing) and we'll follow up, or browse our full list of [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/). Renters open to neighboring options may also want to check [houses for rent in Parma](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-parma-ohio/).

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent a house near Parma Heights?
body: Parma Heights doesn't have its own published rent index, so renters generally reference the wider Cleveland-area market. Zumper's July 4, 2026 report placed the area's median asking rent at $1,250 a month, up 4.2% year-over-year, with two-bedroom homes around $1,100 and three-bedroom homes around $1,350. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, which weights toward larger professionally-managed apartment communities, found a higher average of $1,564 for 787 square feet. An individual Parma Heights house's rent will depend on its size, age, and condition.

Parma Heights is a short highway drive from downtown Cleveland via I-71 or I-480, with Pearl Road (State Route 42) and York Road serving as its main commercial corridors. GCRTA, the region's public transit agency, operates bus routes linking Parma Heights to the wider Cuyahoga County network and Cleveland's west side, though the suburb has no direct RTA rail line.

items: **Highways:** I-71 and I-480, a short drive from downtown Cleveland
**Main corridors:** Pearl Road (SR-42) and York Road
**Transit:** GCRTA bus routes; no direct rail line

sections.h2: Section 8 and housing vouchers in Parma Heights
body: As in the rest of Ohio, there's no state law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8), and Parma Heights is not currently one of the Cuyahoga County suburbs — Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale — that have passed their own source-of-income protection ordinance as of July 2026. Confirm a listing's voucher policy directly before applying. CMHA administers the county's Housing Choice Voucher program, including for renters looking in Parma Heights, and its [preliminary application](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) stays open year-round with placement by rolling lottery rather than strict first-come-first-served. Every home we work with accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready — see our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the full picture.

Electric service in Parma Heights generally comes from The Illuminating Company (FirstEnergy) rather than Cleveland Public Power, which mainly serves addresses inside Cleveland's city limits. Natural gas is typically provided by Enbridge Gas Ohio (formerly Dominion East Ohio), though some Cuyahoga County suburbs use Columbia Gas of Ohio instead — confirm which one serves your address. Cleveland Water, one of the country's largest municipal water systems, supplies many suburbs in addition to the city itself.

sections.h2: Climate and moving considerations near Parma Heights
body: Parma Heights sits on Cleveland's southwest side, outside the region's heaviest lake-effect snow belt, which concentrates in the higher-elevation areas south and east of Lake Erie. The Cleveland area overall averages roughly 60–70 inches of snow a season, with lake-effect activity heaviest from November through January, but southwest suburbs like Parma Heights typically see less accumulation than communities on the city's higher-elevation East Side. Renters moving in winter should still plan for regular snow removal and confirm in the lease whether it's the landlord's or tenant's responsibility.

faq.q: Are there Section 8 houses for rent in Parma Heights?
faq.a: Some landlords accept vouchers, but Ohio law doesn't require it and Parma Heights hasn't passed its own source-of-income protection ordinance, so acceptance varies by listing. CMHA administers the county's Housing Choice Voucher program and can help you search. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready — contact us about homes we work with nearby.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house near Parma Heights?
faq.a: Parma Heights doesn't publish its own rent index, so the closest benchmark is the wider Cleveland market. Zumper's July 2026 report puts the area median around $1,250 a month, while RentCafe's professionally-managed-apartment average runs higher, near $1,564. An individual Parma Heights house's rent depends on its size, age, and condition.

Parma Heights borders Parma, one of Cuyahoga County's largest suburbs, and sits south/southwest of downtown Cleveland along corridors like Pearl Road and York Road. Renters comparing the two suburbs will find a similar mix of single-family homes and smaller rental duplexes in both.

faq.q: Is Parma Heights served by RTA rail?
faq.a: No. Parma Heights has no direct RTA rail line; GCRTA bus routes connect it to the wider Cuyahoga County transit network, and it's a short highway drive from downtown Cleveland via I-71 or I-480, making a car the most practical way to get around.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with homes in Parma Heights?
faq.a: Not currently — our confirmed inventory is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side, with a smaller west side presence. Contact us and tell us you're looking near Parma Heights, and we'll follow up about voucher-friendly homes we work with nearby that could fit your search.

faq.q: What are the main roads through Parma Heights?
faq.a: Pearl Road (State Route 42) and York Road are Parma Heights' main commercial corridors, lined with the suburb's everyday shopping. Both connect to neighboring Parma and feed into I-71 and I-480 for a highway commute toward downtown Cleveland, Berea, or the wider Cuyahoga County suburbs without relying on RTA rail.
